title: Document concurrency model and recommended patterns
markdown:
## Goal

Document the concurrency model for B3nd developers: what `receiveIf` does, when to use it, common patterns (read-modify-write loops with retry), and what happens when conflicts occur.

## Background

With `receiveIf` implemented, developers need clear guidance on how to use it. The optimistic locking pattern requires a specific workflow (read with version, modify, write with expectedVersion, handle conflict, retry) that is not self-evident from the API alone.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `skills/b3nd/APP_COOKBOOK.md` has a new "Concurrency Control" section with:
  - Explanation of the optimistic locking model
  - Code example: read-modify-write loop with retry
  - Code example: first-write-wins pattern (create-if-not-exists)
  - Guidance on when NOT to use receiveIf (append-only patterns, content-addressed data)
- [ ] `skills/b3nd/FRAMEWORK.md` concurrency section updated with receiveIf semantics
- [ ] Version conflict error format documented (`{ accepted: false, error: "version_conflict", version: N }`)

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `skills/b3nd/APP_COOKBOOK.md` to understand existing pattern documentation style
2. Add a "Concurrency Control" section covering:
   - The problem: lost updates in concurrent writes
   - The solution: `receiveIf(msg, { expectedVersion })`
   - Pattern: optimistic read-modify-write with retry
   - Pattern: conditional create (first-write-wins)
   - When to skip: content-addressed (hash://) data is inherently conflict-free
3. Read `skills/b3nd/FRAMEWORK.md` and add concurrency semantics to the appropriate section

## Files to Create/Modify

- `skills/b3nd/APP_COOKBOOK.md` -- add Concurrency Control section
- `skills/b3nd/FRAMEWORK.md` -- update with receiveIf semantics

## Testing

Review documentation for accuracy against the implemented API.

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G1-A4-02 (all clients implemented)
- **Blocks**: Nothing
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Design `receiveIf(uri, data, { expectedVersion })` — conditional writes with optimistic locking
- [ ] Add version tracking to records (or use content hash as version)
- [ ] Update all client implementations to support conditional writes
- [ ] Document concurrency model and recommended patterns
